# objectForKeyedSubscript(_:)

**Instance Method**

Returns the value’s JavaScript property named with the specified key, allowing subscript syntax.

## Declaration

```swift
func objectForKeyedSubscript(_ key: Any!) -> JSValue!
```

## Parameters

- **key**: The name of a property in the JavaScript object.

## Return Value

The value of the named property, or the JavaScript `undefined` value if no property exists by that name.

## Discussion

This method is equivalent to the [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/forProperty(_:)] method, but provides Objective-C subscripting support.

## Accessing Values with Subscript Syntax

- **objectAtIndexedSubscript(_:)**: Returns the value’s JavaScript property at the specified index, allowing subscript syntax.
- **setObject(_:atIndexedSubscript:)**: Sets the value’s JavaScript property at the specified index, allowing subscript syntax.
- **setObject(_:forKeyedSubscript:)**: Sets the value’s JavaScript property named with the specified key, allowing subscript syntax.
